title: Abutilon excelsior
intro: Abutilon excelsior, also known as the flowering maple or Indian mallow, is a plant species in the mallow family (Malvaceae) native to the tropical and subtropical regions of Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, exhibiting potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in the treatment of various ailments including wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Abutilon excelsior include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, often in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
taxonomy: Abutilon excelsior is classified within the family Malvaceae. It belongs to the genus Abutilon. The species is a type of flowering plant native to South Africa. The classification is part of the larger botanical nomenclature system used to identify and categorize plant species. Abutilon excelsior is further classified within the order Malvales.
uses: Abutilon excelsior, also known as the Australian hibiscus, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. Its leaves and roots are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making them useful in treating skin conditions and wounds. In some parts of Australia, the plant is used to treat fever, coughs, and respiratory issues. However, more research is needed to fully understand its medicinal potential and effectiveness. Further studies are required to validate its uses and ensure its safe application in modern medicine.

Its extracts have been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help in reducing swelling and protecting the body against oxidative stress. Abutilon excelsior has also been used to treat respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant properties.
